executive_summary: The PRD-002 implementation (PyInstaller + GitHub Actions CI binary pipeline) is substantially complete. The `parallax.spec` spec file, the CI workflow `.github/workflows/release-cli.yml`, and the version-baking mechanism (`cli/_version.py`) all meet their requirements. The single blocking gap is FR-2: the `cli-build` dependency group is referenced by the CI workflow (`uv sync --group cli-build`) but is absent from `pyproject.toml`, which would cause every CI build to fail at the dependency-install step. All other FRs and all US acceptance criteria (except those dependent on the missing group) are satisfied.

verification_by_fr.notes: parallax.spec exists at repo root. It sets onefile behaviour (all binaries/zipped data passed directly into EXE without COLLECT), console=True, name='parallax', and lists all required packages in excludes: torch, torchvision, torchaudio, transformers, comfy, comfy_diffusion, cv2, numpy, PIL.

pyproject.toml has no [dependency-groups] entry named 'cli-build'. The CI workflow calls `uv sync --group cli-build --no-group dev` but this group does not exist in pyproject.toml. The pyinstaller>=6.0 dependency is not tracked anywhere. This will cause every CI build job to fail at the install step.
The macOS CI job passes `--target-arch universal2` and renames the output to `dist/parallax-macos-universal`.
Release asset names exactly match the convention: parallax-linux-x86_64, parallax-macos-universal, parallax-windows-x86_64.exe. The softprops/action-gh-release step uploads exactly these filenames.

Linux uses sha256sum producing the required '<hex>  <filename>' format. macOS uses shasum -a 256 (functionally equivalent, same output format). Windows uses Get-FileHash with manual string formatting to produce '<hex>  <filename>' — not CertUtil as specified, but the output format is identical. Minor tool-variant deviation does not affect artifact correctness.

parallax.spec reads pyproject.toml at spec-parse time using tomllib and writes cli/_version.py with __version__ = '<version>'. cli/main.py imports __version__ from cli._version and prints it via --version callback.

verification_by_us.notes: AC01: spec produces single-file EXE. AC02: hiddenimports cover typer/click/rich/httpx runtimes; spec is structured to produce a self-contained binary. AC03: all listed heavy deps are in excludes. AC04: binary size cannot be verified statically, but excludes are comprehensive and should keep the binary well under 50 MB.

AC01 (trigger on v*.*.* and workflow_dispatch): comply. AC02 (three parallel jobs on correct runners): comply. AC03 (install step uses cli-build group): does_not_comply — cli-build group missing from pyproject.toml will cause all three build jobs to fail. AC04 (universal2 flag): comply. AC05 (SHA256 files): comply. AC06 (release job with softprops): comply, all 6 files uploaded. AC07 (needs gate): comply, release job has needs: [linux, macos, windows].
AC01: cli/main.py prints 'parallax {__version__}' (e.g. 'parallax 1.3.0'). AC02: version is written to cli/_version.py by the spec at build time, not looked up from an installed package at runtime.

minor_observations: macOS checksum step uses `shasum -a 256` instead of `sha256sum` as specified in FR-5; both produce identical output format and the difference is immaterial on macOS where sha256sum is not natively available.

Windows checksum step uses `Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256` with manual string formatting instead of `CertUtil` as specified in FR-5; the output format matches `<hex>  <filename>` and CertUtil output requires post-processing anyway — this implementation is actually cleaner.

cli/_version.py exists with __version__ = '1.3.0' committed to the repo, which means it is pre-seeded for development. The spec will overwrite it at build time. This is correct per FR-6 but developers should be aware this file is auto-generated and should not be hand-edited.

The spec's `target_arch=None` default means a local `uv run pyinstaller parallax.spec` on macOS will NOT produce a universal2 binary — only the CI job passes --target-arch universal2. This is by design but worth documenting.
conclusions_and_recommendations: The implementation is production-ready modulo one blocking issue: the `cli-build` dependency group must be added to `pyproject.toml`. Without it, `uv sync --group cli-build` fails and no binaries are ever built by CI. Recommended fix: add `[dependency-groups] cli-build = ['pyinstaller>=6.0']` to pyproject.toml. All other components — the PyInstaller spec, the CI workflow structure, the checksum generation, and the version-baking mechanism — are correctly implemented and meet the PRD requirements.
